justifications.temporal_understanding: The agent initially misinterpreted "next Wednesday" as June 10th, but corrected itself in the next turn to June 3rd, and then correctly understood subsequent date requests.
justifications.negotiation_effectiveness: The agent consistently offered the same two days (June 4th and 5th) as alternatives, even when the user was requesting dates much further out, indicating a lack of effective negotiation for new options.
justifications.conversational_efficiency: The agent made several redundant API calls by re-checking dates it had already confirmed as unavailable, and in Turn 7, it repeated its previous response instead of addressing the user's new request.
justifications.customer_experience: The customer experience was frustrating due to the agent's repeated offers of the same unavailable dates, its failure to acknowledge the user's new requests in Turn 7, and the overall difficulty in finding an appointment.
justifications.recovery_quality: The agent struggled to recover from unavailability, repeatedly offering the same limited options and failing to intelligently expand its search beyond the initial narrow window, leading to user frustration and repeated efforts to guide the agent.

judge_scores.overall_notes: The agent struggled significantly with finding available appointments and its negotiation strategy was poor, repeatedly offering the same two days even when the user was pushing for dates weeks later. The conversational flow was also hampered by repeated responses and redundant checks.
